notes.anchor: I seem plainly to have been relegated
notes.note: Relegatus puns on relegatio, the milder Roman penalty of banishment to a fixed place without loss of citizenship. Cicero feels exiled to his Formian villa precisely because, away from Antium, he no longer gets Atticus's flow of Roman news.

notes.anchor: the day before the Nones of May
notes.note: A Roman date formula (pridie Nonas Maias). The Nones fell on the 7th in May, so "the day before" is 6 May; pridie was the regular way to name the day immediately preceding a fixed point.

Odysseus's praise of his homeland Ithaca --- rugged but a good nurse of men, and dearest of all to him. Cicero quotes it ruefully of his own Arpinum, too rough a spot to which to invite Atticus. The line reference is editorial.
